meta.source: Hana-san directive: refine pr-split, automated splitting with Claude Code integration, deep integration tests, cull AI slop
meta.maintenance: Update incrementally after every sub-task. Deviations logged within the plan.

globalAlerts: ALL checks must pass: make (build + lint + test). No exceptions.
Platform compat: Linux, macOS, Windows. Tests use t.TempDir(), t.Setenv().
Integration test config via TestMain flags, project.mk targets with ?= variables.
No estimates, no priorities — 100% completion required.
DO NOT modify unrelated code. DO NOT remove existing comments/documentation.
Auto-split TUI must not hang. Cancellation must propagate immediately.
mandatoryDirectives: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at session start.
Update blueprint.json status after every sub-task.
Checkpoint WIP.md after every string of changes.
Rule of Two: 2 contiguous issue-free review cycles before commit.

statusSection.goalState: osm pr-split provides a fully functional automated splitting pipeline where: (1) `claude spawn` + `claude` works without EAGAIN — RunPassthrough defensively sets fd to blocking mode; (2) MCP session pre-registration is robust — reportClassification auto-creates sessions if missing; (3) Enter key delivery to Claude is reliable via single-write with \n appended; (4) terminal mux re-renders cleanly after toggle via in-memory VT100 virtual screen buffer; (5) integration tests with real AI (via TestMain + project.mk) cover spawn→switch→send→classify end-to-end; (6) all existing tests pass; (7) permission popups are handled via --dangerously-skip-permissions.
statusSection.currentState: T001-T037 marked Done but manual testing reveals CRITICAL runtime failures: (A) 'read /dev/stdin: resource temporarily unavailable' EAGAIN on RunPassthrough — stdin fd in non-blocking mode, no defense in mux.go; (B) MCP 'session not found' error despite pre-registration code; (C) Enter key delivery unreliable; (D) mux terminal doesn't re-render cleanly on toggle. REPLAN TRIGGERED.
replanLog: T006-T009 discovered already implemented (TestMain, integration tests, project.mk all existed). T013 simplified to loadStrategyPlugin removal only.
T021-T025 added: fix handle.send() blocking, fix ctrl+] blank terminal, sendToHandle wrapper, integration tests for new code.
T026-T028 added: fix PTY deadlock root cause (Write mutex), add timeout guard to sendWithCancel, termtest PTY integration tests.
T029-T042 added: fix MCP session pre-registration (BLOCKER), fix Enter key two-write pattern (BLOCKER), add --dangerously-skip-permissions (BLOCKER), update all affected tests, end-to-end validation.

T038-T053 REPLAN: Manual testing revealed T029-T037 fixes are insufficient. EAGAIN on stdin (non-blocking fd), MCP session still not found, Enter key unreliable, mux rendering broken. Complete rewrite of affected areas with integration test coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix auto-split TUI blocking: ensure finishTUI() calls autoSplitTUI.quit() to unblock the BubbleTea program when pipeline completes or errors
sequentialTasks.description: The auto-split TUI hangs after the pipeline finishes because finishTUI() calls autoSplitTUI.done() and autoSplitTUI.wait() but the done message only triggers tea.Quit when the user was already cancelled. When the pipeline finishes normally (or with error), the user sees the done summary but pressing 'q' may not properly exit. Root causes: (1) In autosplit.go, AutoSplitDoneMsg handler only calls tea.Quit if wasCancelled is true; otherwise it stays visible expecting q/Enter. (2) In pr_split_script.js, finishTUI calls wait() which blocks forever if the TUI doesn't exit. (3) The 'q' key handler when done=true sets quitting=true and returns tea.Quit -- this SHOULD work, but the blocking wait() in the JS script may not properly receive the quit signal if the go-prompt is not reading. Fix: ensure the done-state q handler works, and that wait() properly returns after tea program exits.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Running `auto-split` in the TUI, the pipeline shows progress. When complete (or on error or cancellation), pressing q or Enter returns the user to the pr-split command prompt. No hanging. The command loop resumes.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix auto-split cancellation: ensure 'q' during pipeline sets cancelled flag AND the pipeline goroutine checks it between poll intervals
sequentialTasks.description: Currently the first q press sets m.cancelled=true but keep the TUI visible. The pipeline JS checks isCancelled() in pollForFile() and between steps. However, pollForFile uses timemod.sleep() which is a blocking Go sleep -- it does NOT check cancellation during the sleep. The pipeline can be stuck in a sleep(500ms) or a handle.send() and never see the cancelled flag. Fix: (1) Make pollForFile check cancelled BEFORE sleeping. (2) Ensure the JS automatedSplit function checks cancellation after every blocking call (spawn, send, pollForFile). (3) When cancelled is true, finishTUI should call autoSplitTUI.quit() to force the TUI to exit.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Pressing q during an auto-split pipeline cancels within 1 second. The pipeline stops, displays 'Cancelled' status, and returns to the command prompt.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix ctrl+] toggle in auto-split TUI: ensure the OnToggle callback actually invokes tuiMux.RunPassthrough correctly
sequentialTasks.description: In pr_split.go, the autoSplitModel is created with WithAutoSplitOnToggle that calls tuiMux.RunPassthrough(ctx). However, this fails if no child is attached to tuiMux (returns ErrNoChild). The claude command handler attaches/detaches the handle around switchToClaude(), but auto-split spawns Claude separately and may not attach the handle to tuiMux. Fix: (1) In the auto-split pipeline, after spawning Claude successfully, attach the handle to tuiMux. (2) Ensure OnToggle only calls RunPassthrough if a child is attached. (3) After the toggle returns, resume the auto-split TUI (which it already does via AutoSplitToggleMsg).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: During auto-split with a real Claude process, pressing ctrl+] switches to Claude's TUI. Pressing ctrl+] again returns to the auto-split progress view. The pipeline continues running in the background during the toggle.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix auto-split TUI to properly release alt-screen before RunPassthrough and restore after
sequentialTasks.description: The auto-split model uses tea.WithAltScreen(). When the user toggles to Claude via ctrl+], the BubbleTea program is still in alt-screen mode. RunPassthrough enters raw mode and forwards to Claude. When toggling back, the alt-screen needs to be restored. Currently, the toggle handler returns a tea.Cmd that calls m.onToggle() which blocks. This means BubbleTea is blocked in Update. On return, it sends AutoSplitToggleMsg which triggers a re-render. But the alt-screen may not be properly restored because BubbleTea's internal state isn't updated. Need to verify this works correctly or add explicit alt-screen save/restore around the passthrough.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Toggling between auto-split and Claude via ctrl+] renders both UIs cleanly without screen artifacts or cursor positioning issues.

sequentialTasks.task: Attach Claude handle to tuiMux in auto-split pipeline so ctrl+] works
sequentialTasks.description: In pr_split_script.js automatedSplit(), after spawning Claude and getting handle, call tuiMux.attach(handle._goHandle || handle) so that ctrl+] toggle in the auto-split TUI can actually forward to Claude. Must also detach on cleanup. The handle from claudeExecutor needs to be passed through the JS-Go bridge correctly. Check that the _goHandle pattern used in pr_split.go tuiMux.attach logic correctly unwraps the handle.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: After 'Spawn Claude' step succeeds in auto-split, the Claude handle is attached to tuiMux. Pressing ctrl+] during subsequent steps forwards stdin/stdout to Claude. On pipeline completion, the handle is detached.

sequentialTasks.task: Add TestMain-based integration test infrastructure to internal/command for pr-split
sequentialTasks.description: The pr-split package (internal/command) does not have a TestMain. Need to add one that parses -integration, -claude-command, -claude-arg (repeatable), -integration-model flags. When -integration is false (default), all integration tests skip. This mirrors the pattern in internal/builtin/claudemux/integration_test.go. Create a new file internal/command/pr_split_integration_test.go with: (1) TestMain parsing flags, (2) helper functions for git repo setup, (3) skipIfNotIntegration guard.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Running `go test ./internal/command/...` without -integration skips integration tests. Running with `-integration -claude-command=ollama -claude-arg=launch -claude-arg=claude -claude-arg=--model=minimax-m2.5:cloud -claude-arg=--` enables them.

sequentialTasks.task: Create foundational integration test: TestIntegration_AutoSplitHeuristic (no AI, real git)
sequentialTasks.description: Write an integration test that: (1) Creates a temporary git repo with t.TempDir(), (2) Makes an initial commit with several files across dirs, (3) Creates a feature branch with changes (additions, modifications, deletions), (4) Runs the heuristic split (no Claude) via pr_split_script.js, (5) Verifies branches are created, tree-hash equivalence passes, and no content is lost. This tests the real git workflow end-to-end without requiring AI. Uses the Goja engine to execute pr_split_script.js directly.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test creates real branches in a temp git repo, verifies tree-hash equivalence, and confirms all split branches contain the expected files. Test passes on all platforms.

sequentialTasks.task: Create AI integration test: TestIntegration_AutoSplitClaude
sequentialTasks.description: Write an integration test that runs the full automated split pipeline with actual AI (configured via TestMain flags). (1) Creates a complex temp git repo: Go project with multiple packages, imports between them, test files, docs, config. (2) Creates a feature branch with 15-20 changed files across multiple concerns. (3) Invokes automatedSplit(). (4) Verifies: Claude classifies files, plan is generated, branches created, tree-hash equivalent, splits are logically coherent. Uses timeout/skip for CI environments. Tests the MCP result directory flow.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test passes with real AI (ollama or claude), producing split branches that are tree-hash equivalent. Test logs classification and plan details. Test skips gracefully when -integration not set or AI not available.

sequentialTasks.task: Add project.mk integration-test-prsplit target with proper ?= variable defaults
sequentialTasks.description: Update the existing integration-test-prsplit target in project.mk to: (1) Use the new TestMain flags for internal/command (-integration, -claude-command, -claude-arg, -integration-model). (2) Default CLAUDE_COMMAND ?= claude, CLAUDE_ARGS ?= (empty), INTEGRATION_MODEL ?= gpt-oss:20b-cloud. (3) Support config.mk local overrides. Ensure the target properly passes multiple -claude-arg flags using $(foreach).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make integration-test-prsplit` runs pr-split integration tests. `make integration-test-prsplit CLAUDE_COMMAND=ollama CLAUDE_ARGS='launch claude --model=minimax-m2.5:cloud --'` works correctly. config.mk can override defaults.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix ClaudeCodeExecutor.spawn() to work with the user's command pattern: -claude-command=ollama -claude-arg=launch -claude-arg=claude
sequentialTasks.description: The user runs `osm pr-split -claude-command=ollama -claude-arg=launch -claude-arg=claude -claude-arg=--model=minimax-m2.5:cloud -claude-arg=--`. The executor currently resolves 'ollama' as provider type 'ollama' and constructs spawn opts with the args. But the spawn logic then uses cm.ollama() which creates an OllamaProvider that may append its own args. Need to verify the arg passing chain: (1) prSplitConfig.claudeArgs contains ['launch', 'claude', '--model=minimax-m2.5:cloud', '--']. (2) These are passed through to the spawn correctly. (3) The MCP config args are appended AFTER the user's args. Test by tracing the command construction.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: When run with -claude-command=ollama -claude-arg=launch -claude-arg=claude -claude-arg=--model=minimax-m2.5:cloud -claude-arg=--, the executor spawns: ollama launch claude --model=minimax-m2.5:cloud -- --mcp-config=<path>. The process starts successfully.
sequentialTasks.status: Done (verified correct — traced full arg chain through JS→Go→PTY)

sequentialTasks.task: Add integration test for cancellation: TestIntegration_AutoSplitCancel
sequentialTasks.description: Test that the auto-split pipeline can be cancelled mid-operation. (1) Create a temp git repo. (2) Start auto-split in a goroutine. (3) After the 'Analyze diff' step completes, trigger cancellation via autoSplitTUI.quit(). (4) Verify the pipeline returns with a cancellation error within 2 seconds. (5) Verify the original branch is restored. This tests the cooperative cancellation flow end-to-end.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test demonstrates that cancellation during auto-split terminates the pipeline, restores the git state, and returns control within a reasonable timeout.

sequentialTasks.task: Ensure auto-split properly restores original branch on all exit paths
sequentialTasks.description: Review automatedSplit() and executeSplit() to ensure that on every exit path (success, error, cancellation, timeout), the original branch is restored via git checkout. Currently executeSplit() saves and restores the branch, but automatedSplit() may not if an error occurs between steps (e.g., after classification but before execution). Add a defer-like pattern in the JS (try/finally) to restore the branch.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: After any auto-split invocation (success, failure, cancel), `git branch --show-current` returns the original branch. No orphaned checkout state.

sequentialTasks.task: Clean up AI slop: remove telemetry, plugin system, and retrospective code that isn't wired up or tested
sequentialTasks.description: The pr_split_script.js contains several features that are 'AI slop' per the project standards: (1) saveTelemetry() uses echo+redirect to write files -- fragile and untested. (2) loadStrategyPlugin() uses eval() -- security risk, never tested in any test file. (3) analyzeRetrospective() has no integration test and marginal value. However, per Hana's instructions, DO NOT remove code that serves a clear purpose. These should be evaluated: telemetry command IS registered and functional; retro command IS registered. Per the definition: code that is 'purpose-less' and 'untested'. Telemetry and retro DO have tests (the TUI commands exist). loadStrategyPlugin with eval() IS a security concern. DECISION: Remove loadStrategyPlugin (uses eval, no tests). Keep telemetry and retro (they're wired up as TUI commands).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: loadStrategyPlugin is removed from pr_split_script.js. All references to it in the exports object are removed. No test breakage.

sequentialTasks.task: Verify and fix the auto-split output pane scroll behavior after cancellation
sequentialTasks.description: When the user presses q during auto-split, the first press sets cancelled=true and shows 'Cancelling...' in the separator bar. The pipeline should detect this and call finishTUI with an error. Then the done summary should appear. But the scroll offset might be stale, and the View() method should render the 'Cancelling...' state correctly. Review the separator rendering in renderSeparator() to confirm it shows the right state.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The auto-split TUI correctly shows 'Cancelling...' after first q press, then shows the done summary after the pipeline finishes cleaning up. The output pane scrolls correctly throughout.
sequentialTasks.status: Done (pre-existing — scroll keys work in all states)

sequentialTasks.task: Add unit test for auto-split model cancellation-then-done sequence
sequentialTasks.description: Write a test in autosplit_test.go that: (1) Creates an AutoSplitModel, (2) Sends StepStartMsg for a step, (3) Sends a key 'q' message (first cancel), (4) Verifies cancelled=true but quitting=false, (5) Sends AutoSplitDoneMsg (pipeline acknowledges cancel), (6) Verifies the model calls tea.Quit. This tests the specific scenario where the pipeline detects cancellation and finishes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test verifies the two-phase cancellation dance: q sets cancelled, done-msg triggers quit. The model transitions cleanly.

sequentialTasks.task: Add unit test for auto-split toggle key dispatching with OnToggle callback
sequentialTasks.description: Write a test in autosplit_test.go that verifies pressing ctrl+] dispatches AutoSplitToggleMsg via the OnToggle callback. The test should: (1) Create a model with a toggle callback that records it was called, (2) Send the ctrl+] key, (3) Verify the callback was invoked, (4) Verify the model handled AutoSplitToggleMsg correctly.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test confirms ctrl+] key triggers the toggle callback and the model correctly handles the return message.

sequentialTasks.task: Verify existing tests pass after all changes
sequentialTasks.description: Run the full test suite with `make test` to confirm no regressions. Fix any test failures introduced by the changes. Run `make lint` to confirm no lint errors.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make` completes with zero failures on macOS.

sequentialTasks.task: Create complex integration test scenario: TestIntegration_AutoSplitComplexGoProject
sequentialTasks.description: Create an integration test that mimics a real-world Go project PR split scenario: (1) Temp repo with 3+ Go packages (internal/foo, internal/bar, internal/baz), (2) Go module with inter-package imports, (3) Feature branch adds new package + modifies 2 existing + adds tests + updates docs, (4) Run auto-split with AI, (5) Verify: each split branch builds independently (`go build ./...`), tests pass on each branch, splits are logically grouped (config changes separate from feature code), tree-hash equivalence confirmed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test creates a realistic Go project, splits it with AI into coherent groups, and verifies each split branch independently builds and tests pass. Documents the classification and plan in test output.

sequentialTasks.task: Ensure finishTUI in auto-split properly handles the case where TUI was never started
sequentialTasks.description: If auto-split runs without a TUI (e.g., in test mode or non-interactive), finishTUI should be a no-op. Currently has hasTUI guard, but verify all paths are safe. The config.disableTUI flag should be used in tests.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Auto-split works correctly both with and without TUI. Tests can run auto-split without starting a BubbleTea program.
sequentialTasks.status: Done (pre-existing — hasTUI guard handles all paths)

sequentialTasks.task: Add comprehensive error messages when Claude spawn fails with specific command patterns
sequentialTasks.description: When the user runs with -claude-command=ollama -claude-arg=launch -claude-arg=claude etc., failure messages should be clear about what was attempted and why it failed. Show the full command that was attempted, the exit code, and stderr. Currently errors may be generic.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: When Claude spawn fails, the error message shows the exact command attempted and the failure reason.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix handle.send() blocking: extract prSplitSendWithCancel as standalone testable Go function
sequentialTasks.description: handle.send() is a synchronous PTY write that blocks when the child's read buffer is full, freezing the JS thread and preventing cancellation. Extracted standalone prSplitSendWithCancel(send, kill, cancelled, forceCancelled) function at end of pr_split.go. Runs send() in goroutine, polls cancel/forceCancel every 200ms via ticker, calls kill() (SIGKILL) on cancel to unblock the write. The autoSplitTUI map's sendWithCancel entry delegates to this function after extracting the Go handle via extractGoHandle.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: prSplitSendWithCancel is a standalone, exported function with injectable dependencies. Tests (Cancel, ForceCancel, Success, RealPTYKill) verify all paths. No goroutine leaks. No deadlocks.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix ctrl+] blank terminal: add HasChild() guard to TUIMux and check before RunPassthrough
sequentialTasks.description: ctrl+] calls RunPassthrough even when no child is attached, resulting in a blank terminal with no way back. Added HasChild() bool method to TUIMux (mutex-protected m.child != nil check). The OnToggle callback in pr_split.go now checks HasChild() before calling ReleaseTerminal/RunPassthrough, showing an error via SendError if no child is attached. Also captures RunPassthrough error and reports via SendError.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Pressing ctrl+] when no Claude process is attached shows an error message instead of blanking the screen. When a child IS attached, toggle works as before. TestHasChild verifies the false→true→false lifecycle.

sequentialTasks.task: Add sendToHandle JS wrapper for cancellation-aware send across all pipeline call sites
sequentialTasks.description: Added sendToHandle(handle, text) function in pr_split_script.js that uses autoSplitTUI.sendWithCancel when available (TUI mode) or falls back to direct handle.send() in test/non-TUI contexts. Returns { error: null } on success, { error: 'message' } on failure. Replaced all 4 handle.send() call sites in the automated pipeline: classification prompt, conflict fix, re-classify, resolveConflictsWithClaude. Exported as globalThis.prSplit.sendToHandle.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 4 send sites use sendToHandle. Error semantics preserved (early return on error). TestIntegration_SendToHandle_FallbackDirect and TestIntegration_SendToHandle_FallbackError verify both paths.

sequentialTasks.task: Improve cleanupExecutor: SIGKILL before close on forceCancel, retry detach after close
sequentialTasks.description: cleanupExecutor() now: (1) tracks whether first detach() succeeded, (2) on forceCancel sends SIGKILL before close() to skip the 5s graceful SIGTERM→wait→SIGKILL shutdown, (3) retries detach after close if initial detach failed (passthrough should have exited after child EOF). All operations are best-effort with try/catch.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Force-cancel terminates Claude immediately without the 5-second wait. Detach succeeds even if passthrough was active at cleanup time.

sequentialTasks.task: Write comprehensive integration tests for sendWithCancel, cancel, forceCancel, and real PTY kill
sequentialTasks.description: Added 7 tests: (1) SendToHandle_FallbackDirect — no TUI, direct send, (2) SendToHandle_FallbackError — error propagation, (3) NormalWrite — real cat PTY happy path, (4) Cancel — synthetic blocking send + cancel flag, (5) ForceCancel — synthetic blocking send + forceCancel flag, (6) RealPTYKill — real sleep PTY + SIGKILL, (7) AutoSplitWithClaude_Pipeline — full AI integration test gated by -integration flag. Synthetic tests avoid platform-specific PTY buffer size issues (macOS buffers >1MB).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 7 tests pass on macOS (and should pass on Linux). Cancel/ForceCancel tests verify completion within 2s. RealPTYKill verifies SIGKILL works with real processes. AutoSplitWithClaude_Pipeline is ready for real Claude execution.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix PTY deadlock: Process.Write() must release mutex before blocking kernel I/O
sequentialTasks.description: ROOT CAUSE of all auto-split hanging/cancellation bugs. Process.Write() held p.mu during p.ptyFile.Write() — a blocking kernel call. When the PTY buffer fills (Claude hasn't started reading stdin), Write blocks WHILE HOLDING THE LOCK. Then Signal/Close try to acquire the same mutex → deadlock. Fix: save p.ptyFile under lock, release lock, then do I/O — matching the pattern already used by Read(). Also added fallback direct syscall.Kill(pid, SIGKILL) in Close() for zombie-state processes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestProcess_WriteSignalDeadlock proves Signal completes while Write is blocked. TestProcess_CloseWhileWriteBlocked proves Close completes. No mutex held during blocking I/O in Write().

sequentialTasks.task: Add timeout guard to prSplitSendWithCancel after kill()
sequentialTasks.description: Defense in depth: after calling kill() (SIGKILL), prSplitSendWithCancel now has a 10-second timeout on <-done instead of blocking forever. If the send goroutine doesn't return within 10s post-kill, the function returns with an error instead of hanging.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: prSplitSendWithCancel never blocks indefinitely after kill(). Timeout fires after 10s as fallback.

sequentialTasks.task: Write termtest PTY integration tests for auto-split cancel during blocked send and end-to-end flow
sequentialTasks.description: Created pr_split_pty_unix_test.go with three termtest-based tests: (1) TestPTY_AutoSplit_SendBlockedCancelWorks — tests prSplitSendWithCancel directly with a blocked PTY write to sleep(3600), verifies cancel terminates within 10s. (2) TestPTY_AutoSplit_EndToEnd — spawns real osm binary with mock Claude script, triggers auto-split, verifies TUI renders, sends q to cancel, verifies prompt returns. (3) TestPTY_AutoSplit_CancelDuringBlockedSend — spawns osm with sleep-only mock Claude (never reads stdin), triggers auto-split, waits for Send classification step, sends q to cancel blocked send, verifies the deadlock fix allows clean cancellation. Uses WriteString for raw character sending, not Send (which is for named keys only). Added test-pr-split-pty make target in config.mk.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 3 tests pass on macOS/Linux. CancelDuringBlockedSend specifically validates the deadlock fix in a realistic scenario with a real PTY, BubbleTea TUI, and send cancellation.

sequentialTasks.task: Add initialSessionID parameter to newMCPServer for session pre-registration
sequentialTasks.description: In mcp.go, newMCPServer (line 351) creates empty sessions map. reportClassification (line 878) fails with 'session not found'. Fix: add initialSessionID string param; if non-empty, pre-populate sessions map with idle session.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: When osm mcp-instance --session prsplit-123 starts, the sessions map contains prsplit-123. Claude can immediately call reportClassification without registerSession.

sequentialTasks.task: Wire mcp_instance.go to pass c.session as initialSessionID to newMCPServer
sequentialTasks.description: mcp_instance.go Execute (line 71) calls newMCPServer without session. After T029, pass c.session. Also update standalone MCPCommand caller to pass empty string.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: mcp_instance.go passes c.session through. Standalone osm mcp passes empty string. Both compile.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix sendToHandle: two-write pattern — prompt text first, then \r separately
sequentialTasks.description: sendToHandle (line 2084) currently appends \n inline. Claude Code in raw mode expects \r as Enter. Fix: do TWO writes — first text, then \r. Both autoSplitTUI.sendWithCancel and handle.send() fallback paths.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: After sendToHandle, PTY receives two writes: prompt text then \r. Claude Code submits the prompt.

sequentialTasks.task: Add --dangerously-skip-permissions to auto-split Claude spawn args
sequentialTasks.description: ClaudeCodeExecutor.spawn (line 1838) constructs args. Prepend --dangerously-skip-permissions before user args and --mcp-config. Only for claude-code/explicit types, not ollama.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Auto-split Claude spawn includes --dangerously-skip-permissions. No permission popups block the pipeline. Ollama spawn is unaffected.

sequentialTasks.task: Update SendToHandle tests for two-write pattern and \r Enter key
sequentialTasks.description: TestIntegration_SendToHandle_FallbackDirect and FallbackError must be updated. Mock handle records array of sends. Assert two writes: text then \r. Error test covers first-send and second-send failure paths.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Both tests pass verifying two-write pattern with \r Enter key.

sequentialTasks.task: Update MCP test helpers to pass initialSessionID parameter
sequentialTasks.description: mcp_test.go helpers (newMCPTestEnv, newMCPTestEnvWithResultDir) call newMCPServer. Update to pass empty string for backward compat.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All existing MCP tests compile and pass unchanged.

sequentialTasks.task: Add MCP test: pre-registered session allows reportClassification without registerSession
sequentialTasks.description: New test creates MCP server with initialSessionID, calls reportClassification directly, asserts success. Also asserts different sessionId still fails.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Pre-registered sessions work immediately. Non-pre-registered sessions still require registerSession.

sequentialTasks.task: Add unit test for --dangerously-skip-permissions in spawn args
sequentialTasks.description: Test that ClaudeCodeExecutor.spawn constructs args with --dangerously-skip-permissions first, before user args and --mcp-config.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test proves --dangerously-skip-permissions is first arg in spawn args array.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full make and fix any compilation or test failures from T029-T036
sequentialTasks.description: Run make (build + lint + test). Fix all errors. Ensure all new code is wired up (no deadcode). All existing tests pass.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: make completes with zero errors. All tests pass. No lint warnings.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix EAGAIN: Add ensureBlocking/restoreBlocking to RunPassthrough that explicitly sets stdin fd to blocking mode before reading
sequentialTasks.description: ROOT CAUSE: os.Stdin.Read() in RunPassthrough's goroutine returns EAGAIN because stdin's fd is in non-blocking mode (set by go-prompt, BubbleTea cancelreader, or Go runtime). Go's runtime does NOT retry EAGAIN for TTY fds — it surfaces the error directly. Fix in internal/termui/mux/mux.go: (1) Create mux_blocking_unix.go with ensureBlockingFd(fd int) (origFlags int, err error) using syscall.Fcntl(fd, syscall.F_GETFL, 0) then syscall.Fcntl(fd, syscall.F_SETFL, origFlags &^ syscall.O_NONBLOCK). (2) Create restoreBlockingFd(fd int, origFlags int) error. (3) Create mux_blocking_other.go for Windows with no-op stubs. (4) In RunPassthrough, after term.MakeRaw(termFd) succeeds, call ensureBlockingFd(termFd). Defer restoreBlockingFd. (5) ALSO add EAGAIN retry in the stdin→child goroutine as defense-in-depth: if errors.Is(err, syscall.EAGAIN) || errors.Is(err, syscall.EWOULDBLOCK), call runtime.Gosched() and continue instead of sending ExitError.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: After fix, `claude spawn` followed by `claude` in pr-split enters RunPassthrough without EAGAIN error. stdin reads block correctly waiting for user input. The user sees Claude's TUI output and can press Ctrl+] to toggle back. Test: unit test with a pipe set to O_NONBLOCK passed as stdin verifies ensureBlockingFd restores blocking mode and Read succeeds.

sequentialTasks.task: Add unit test for EAGAIN retry in RunPassthrough reader goroutine
sequentialTasks.description: In mux_test.go, add TestRunPassthrough_EAGAINRetry: (1) Create a custom io.Reader that returns syscall.EAGAIN on the first 3 reads, then returns valid data, then returns toggle key. (2) Create TUIMux with this custom reader and a pipe as child. (3) Call RunPassthrough. (4) Verify it returns ExitToggle (not ExitError). This proves the EAGAIN retry works even if ensureBlockingFd fails to clear non-blocking. Also add TestEnsureBlockingFd (Unix only) that creates a pipe, sets it to O_NONBLOCK, calls ensureBlockingFd, and verifies reads block correctly.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestRunPassthrough_EAGAINRetry passes on all platforms. TestEnsureBlockingFd passes on Unix (skips on Windows). Both tests verify the defense-in-depth layers independently.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix Enter key: add 50ms delay between text and Enter writes in sendToHandle
sequentialTasks.description: Added 50ms timemod.sleep() delay between the prompt text write and the Enter (\r) write in sendToHandle. Both TUI and fallback paths have the delay. This gives the PTY time to process the paste before seeing CR as an independent keypress.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: sendToHandle has a 50ms delay between text and Enter writes. Existing tests still pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Update sendToHandle tests for two-write pattern with 50ms delay

sequentialTasks.acceptance: Both tests pass verifying two-write pattern. No test changes needed — delay is transparent.

Validates the complete Claude Code integration path.

sequentialTasks.task: Add integration test: TestIntegration_ClaudeSpawnSwitch — verify spawn→switch→toggle flow with real PTY
sequentialTasks.description: In pr_split_pty_unix_test.go: (1) Build osm binary. (2) Create a mock 'claude' script that starts a simple shell (cat or bash). (3) Spawn osm pr-split with --claude-command pointing to the mock script. (4) Wait for go-prompt to be ready (detect '(pr-split) >' prompt). (5) Send 'claude spawn\n'. (6) Verify 'Claude spawned' output. (7) Send 'claude\n'. (8) Verify 'Switching to Claude TUI' output. (9) Verify NO 'resource temporarily unavailable' error. (10) Send Ctrl+] to toggle back. (11) Verify 'Back to osm' output. Uses termtest or raw PTY process. Unix-only (build tag). This test SPECIFICALLY validates the EAGAIN fix.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test proves that claude spawn→claude→Ctrl+] toggle cycle works end-to-end in a real PTY without EAGAIN. Test is deterministic (uses sync markers, not sleeps). Passes on macOS and Linux.

sequentialTasks.task: Add integration test: TestIntegration_RealClaude_ClassificationE2E — full pipeline with real AI via TestMain flags
sequentialTasks.description: In pr_split_integration_test.go, gated by -integration flag: (1) Create temp git repo with Go project (3 packages, imports, tests). (2) Create feature branch with 10+ changed files across packages. (3) Build osm binary. (4) Spawn osm pr-split with -claude-command and -claude-arg from TestMain. (5) Send 'auto-split\n'. (6) Wait for auto-split TUI to start. (7) Wait for 'Classify' step to complete (poll MCP result dir for classification.json). (8) Verify classification.json contains all changed files. (9) Wait for 'Plan' step. (10) Send 'q' to cancel after classification (we just need to verify the prompt→Claude→MCP round trip works). Uses -timeout=5m. Logs all output.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test proves the full spawn→send-prompt→Claude-processes→MCP-callback round trip works with real AI. classification.json is created with valid content. Test gracefully handles AI unavailability (skip with message).

sequentialTasks.task: Implement VT100 virtual terminal buffer in internal/termui/mux/vterm.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create a VT100-compatible virtual terminal buffer that maintains an in-memory screen state. Core capabilities: (1) 2D grid of cells, each with character + foreground color + background color + bold/underline/inverse attributes. (2) Cursor tracking (row, col). (3) ANSI CSI sequence parsing: cursor movement (CUU/CUD/CUF/CUB/CUP/HVP), erase (ED/EL/ECH), insert/delete line (IL/DL), scroll region (DECSTBM), character attributes (SGR including 256-color and truecolor). (4) Alt-screen support (DECSET 1049 / DECRST 1049). (5) Line wrapping. (6) Render() method that produces a string with ANSI codes to reproduce the current screen state. (7) Write(p []byte) (n int, err error) io.Writer interface. (8) Resize(rows, cols int). Keep it focused on what Claude Code actually uses — don't over-engineer a full xterm. Use byte-at-a-time state machine parsing.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: VTerm correctly parses and renders: plain text, colored text (SGR), cursor movement sequences, screen clearing (ED), line erasing (EL), scroll regions. VTerm.Render() produces output that, when written to a real terminal, looks identical to the original stream. Unit tests verify each capability independently.

sequentialTasks.task: Integrate VTerm into TUIMux for child output capture and re-render on toggle
sequentialTasks.description: In mux.go: (1) Add childVterm *VTerm field to TUIMux (created on Attach with current terminal dimensions). (2) In RunPassthrough's child→stdout goroutine: tee output to childVterm.Write(buf[:n]) alongside m.stdout.Write(buf[:n]). (3) On toggle BACK from Claude (ExitToggle in RunPassthrough): after restoring terminal state, call childVterm.Render() to nothing (the vterm state is preserved for next toggle). (4) On toggle TO Claude: if childVterm has content, write childVterm.Render() to m.stdout before starting byte forwarding. This ensures Claude's screen is restored when toggling back to it. (5) On Detach: nil out childVterm.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Toggling to Claude (Ctrl+]) shows Claude's last screen state if Claude has previously rendered output. Toggling away and back preserves Claude's display. No screen artifacts from stale content. Unit test with mock child writes to vterm, verifies Render() produces expected output after simulated toggle cycle.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix ReleaseTerminal/RestoreTerminal race: add synchronization channel for alt-screen lifecycle
sequentialTasks.description: In pr_split.go's onToggle callback: ReleaseTerminal() is called but it's async (returns a tea.Cmd). RunPassthrough may start before alt-screen is fully released. Fix: (1) After calling p.ReleaseTerminal(), add a small deterministic wait (50ms) to allow BubbleTea's event loop to process the release. Better: use p.DisableInput() + direct escape sequences to exit alt-screen synchronously (write \x1b[?1049l to stdout). (2) Before calling p.RestoreTerminal(), write \x1b[?1049h to stdout to enter alt-screen synchronously, then call RestoreTerminal to let BubbleTea know. (3) Add \x1b[2J\x1b[H (clear + home) after RestoreTerminal to ensure clean re-render.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Toggling from auto-split to Claude and back produces no screen artifacts. The alt-screen is fully exited before RunPassthrough starts. The auto-split TUI re-renders cleanly on return. No overlapping content from Claude's output on the auto-split screen.

finalEnforcementProtocol: ALL make targets must pass before any commit.
Rule of Two: 2 contiguous subagent reviews required.
Integration tests must be verified with actual AI execution.
No hanging, no blocking, no screen artifacts in TUI.
